title: French environment minister backtracks on resignation over 'urgency' to act on climate

title: Louvre heist gallery reopens to public, but with nothing to steal
subhed: The Louvre Museum's Apollo Gallery reopened for the first time since thieves broke into it and stole jewels in October 2025. It has been emptied of the remaining jewels, which are set to be displayed in a secure space in the museum.

title: Bernard Arnault's business-first strategy with foreign heads of state, from Trump to Putin
subhed: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (3/6). The head of LVMH, which generates 70% of its revenue outside Europe, has fostered relationships with the leaders of the markets he targets. Foremost among them is the president of the United States, whom he has courted since the 1980s.

subhed: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (2/6). LVMH's boss has never hidden his allegiance to France's pro-business, establishment right. He's close to fellow billionaire Vincent Bolloré and, above all, pragmatic. Now he's preparing for the far right's rise to power.

title: Climate scientists aren't surprised by summer's record heat: 'We've entered a dangerous zone'
subhed: Record heat, historic drought and sweeping wildfires have marked France's 2026 summer. Yet climate scientists mostly see global warming's expected effects playing out. Only the rapid succession of heatwaves has caught them off guard.

title: Didier Deschamps bids farewell to French team after spectacular World Cup defeat
subhed: The coach stepped down after a dramatic 6-4 loss in the World Cup third-place match against England on Saturday. Over his 14 years in the role, he nonetheless managed to restore France to the pinnacle of world football.
